Title: Biosynthesis of plant hemostatic dencichine in Escherichia coli

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of serB increased L-DAP supply and ultimately improved de novo β-ODAP production, though with impaired growth.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. serB encodes phosphoserine phosphatase and competes with SbnA for O-phospho-L-serine. 2. Knockout of serB increased L-DAP titer from 316.3 to 842.5 mg/L in sbnAB-expressing strains. 3. In the final production route, serB inactivation enabled β-ODAP to continue accumulating to 387.9 mg/L at 96 h, but reduced biomass.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistically reduces shunt of O-phospho-L-serine to L-serine and may relieve feedback inhibition on serA by L-serine.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hemostatic plant metabolite; platelet aggregation promoter; reported therapeutic relevance to diabetic nephropathy
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful host-engineering target to increase precursor L-DAP flux for β-ODAP biosynthesis, but growth penalty must be balanced.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Shake-flask culture in M9Y medium with glycerol and IPTG induction

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of aceA strongly increased glyoxylate supply and boosted β-ODAP titer to 1289.7 mg/L.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. aceA provides glyoxylate by cleaving isocitrate to succinate and glyoxylate. 2. Overexpression in the optimized producer BW15 generated BW16. 3. BW16 reached 1289.7 mg/L β-ODAP at 96 h with yield 0.28 g/g glycerol.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene overexpression; shake-flask fermentation; HPLC and ESI-MS product verification
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a precursor-supply enhancement step rather than direct β-ODAP synthesis enzyme.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hemostatic plant metabolite; platelet aggregation promoter; reported therapeutic relevance to diabetic nephropathy
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong metabolic engineering lever to enhance glyoxylate precursor supply in microbial β-ODAP production systems.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Shake-flask culture in M9Y medium with glycerol and IPTG induction
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in engineered producer strain BW16

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Artificial β-ODAP biosynthesis; Module I L-DAP biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses glutamate and O-phospho-L-serine to form ACEGA.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate leading to L-DAP, the direct precursor of β-ODAP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with sbnB enabled de novo L-DAP accumulation and supported β-ODAP production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. sbnA and sbnB from the staphyloferrin B pathway were used to reconstruct L-DAP biosynthesis in E. coli. 2. sbnAB expression produced 316.3 mg/L L-DAP. 3. In combination with downstream modules, sbnAB supplied precursor for de novo β-ODAP biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression; shake-flask production; HPLC metabolite analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hemostatic plant metabolite; platelet aggregation promoter; reported therapeutic relevance to diabetic nephropathy
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential upstream module for microbial production of β-ODAP from central metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux competes with native serine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Shake-flask culture in M9Y medium with glycerol and IPTG induction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions as the first committed heterologous step of Module I.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Artificial β-ODAP biosynthesis; Module I L-DAP biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes/dehydrogenates ACEGA to produce L-DAP and 2-oxoglutarate.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms L-DAP, the immediate precursor of β-ODAP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with sbnA enabled high L-DAP accumulation and de novo β-ODAP production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. sbnB acts after sbnA in the microbial L-DAP pathway. 2. sbnAB expression established L-DAP production in E. coli. 3. This module fed the terminal plant acyltransferase step for β-ODAP biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression; shake-flask production; HPLC metabolite analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hemostatic plant metabolite; platelet aggregation promoter; reported therapeutic relevance to diabetic nephropathy
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential upstream enzyme for generating L-DAP precursor in engineered microbial hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Shake-flask culture in M9Y medium with glycerol and IPTG induction

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Artificial β-ODAP biosynthesis; Module II oxalyl-CoA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms the acyl donor used by LsBAHD to produce β-ODAP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled β-ODAP formation when combined with oxalate-producing routes and LsBAHD/LsBAHD*.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ScAAE was selected over AtAAE and native YfdE due to strict oxalate specificity and better catalytic activity. 2. In combination with Scgloxdh or Fpoah and LsBAHD, it supported β-ODAP production in feeding and de novo experiments. 3. It is an essential component of oxalyl-CoA supply.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway assembly; feeding experiments; β-ODAP quantification
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hemostatic plant metabolite; platelet aggregation promoter; reported therapeutic relevance to diabetic nephropathy
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful oxalyl-CoA supply enzyme for synthetic β-ODAP pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dependent on adequate upstream oxalate supply
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding with glyoxylate or oxaloacetate depending on paired upstream module
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chosen based on prior reports cited in paper rather than de novo screening in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome c-dependent oxidoreductase; originally annotated as L-lactate cytochrome c oxidoreductase
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Artificial β-ODAP biosynthesis; Module II oxalyl-CoA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes glyoxylate to oxalate using cytochrome c as electron acceptor.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies oxalate precursor for ScAAE-mediated oxalyl-CoA formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provided a soluble oxalate-producing route that supported stronger β-ODAP production than the PanE route.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cyb2p from S. cerevisiae was discovered to possess glyoxylate dehydrogenase activity and was renamed Scgloxdh. 2. It had slightly lower catalytic efficiency than Fpgloxdh but much better solubility in E. coli. 3. In combination with ScAAE and LsBAHD*, it supported major β-ODAP production improvements, especially after blocking glyoxylate degradation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homolog mining; protein purification; in vitro enzyme assay; pathway assembly; feeding experiments
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hemostatic plant metabolite; platelet aggregation promoter; reported therapeutic relevance to diabetic nephropathy
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good soluble enzyme choice for constructing oxalate supply from glyoxylate in microbial β-ODAP pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Requires cytochrome c-linked oxidation chemistry; efficiency slightly below Fpgloxdh in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding with glyoxylate; enhanced strongly after deletion of glyoxylate-consuming genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Repurposed enzyme with moonlighting aldehyde dehydrogenase activity on glyoxylate.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Artificial β-ODAP biosynthesis; Module II oxalyl-CoA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves oxaloacetate into oxalate and acetate.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies oxalate precursor for ScAAE-mediated oxalyl-CoA formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supported β-ODAP production from oxaloacetate and contributed synergistically with Scgloxdh to improve de novo β-ODAP production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among Oah candidates, Fpoah showed good solubility and favorable kinetics and was selected. 2. Combined with ScAAE and LsBAHD, it enabled β-ODAP production from oxaloacetate feeding. 3. Synergy between Fpoah and Scgloxdh increased de novo β-ODAP titer to 229.5 mg/L before further host engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein purification; in vitro enzyme assay; pathway assembly; feeding and de novo fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hemostatic plant metabolite; platelet aggregation promoter; reported therapeutic relevance to diabetic nephropathy
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful complementary oxalate-supply route that can be combined with glyoxylate oxidation for higher β-ODAP titers.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Standalone route gave lower β-ODAP than glyoxylate oxidation route in feeding experiments
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Oxaloacetate feeding; synergy tested in de novo culture
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected over Pcoah based on lower Km with similar catalytic efficiency.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Artificial β-ODAP biosynthesis; Module II oxalyl-CoA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the reversible interconversion between glyoxylate and oxalyl-CoA in the presence of CoA/NADP+.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms oxalyl-CoA acyl donor for LsBAHD
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In vitro active, but the PanE-based pathway did not support detectable β-ODAP with original LsBAHD and only low production after LsBAHD* and host deletions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PanE showed strong in vitro catalytic efficiency toward glyoxylate. 2. In vivo the PanE-based route was ineffective or weak for β-ODAP production, likely due to reaction reversibility and competition with native glyoxylate metabolism. 3. Glyoxylate degradation deletions improved performance of this route but it still underperformed the Scgloxdh/ScAAE route.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein purification; in vitro assay; pathway assembly; feeding/de novo fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hemostatic plant metabolite; platelet aggregation promoter; reported therapeutic relevance to diabetic nephropathy
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible alternative oxalyl-CoA-forming enzyme, but less favorable than oxidation-based routes in E. coli.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reversible reaction limits in vivo flux; competition with native glyoxylate pathways
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glyoxylate feeding; performance improved after glyoxylate-degradation knockouts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used also in coupled assay to generate oxalyl-CoA for LsBAHD kinetics.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; conserved HXXXD and DFGWG motifs
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Artificial β-ODAP biosynthesis; Module III β-ODAP biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the oxalyl group from oxalyl-CoA to L-DAP to form β-ODAP.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential terminal enzyme for β-ODAP formation; poor solubility created a bottleneck, and synonymous rare-codon engineering markedly increased production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LsBAHD from L. sativus is the last and only plant-derived enzyme used in the engineered β-ODAP pathway. 2. The enzyme specifically catalyzed condensation of oxalyl-CoA and L-DAP to form β-ODAP, with narrow substrate specificity. 3. Poor solubility made it a rate-limiting step; whole-gene isoleucine rare-codon substitution (LsBAHD*) raised solubility ~7-fold and increased β-ODAP production across pathway designs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein expression/solubility analysis; coupled enzyme assay; substrate specificity assay; pathway replacement and fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: rate-limiting step supported by poor solubility and production improvement upon solubility engineering
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hemostatic plant metabolite; platelet aggregation promoter; reported therapeutic relevance to diabetic nephropathy
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core terminal biosynthetic gene for microbial dencichine production; codon/translation tuning is critical for deployment.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Performance evaluated in feeding and de novo shake-flask cultures
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified previously from transcriptome sequencing and analysis of L. sativus
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously overexpressed; original codon-optimized form mostly insoluble
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome sequencing mentioned for prior identification in L. sativus; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only reported oxalyl-CoA transferase for β-ODAP synthesis according to the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; synonymous rare-codon engineered variant
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Artificial β-ODAP biosynthesis; Module III β-ODAP biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Same catalytic reaction as LsBAHD, with synonymous codon substitutions to improve folding/solubility in E. coli.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Approximately seven-fold higher soluble protein level than LsBAHD and significantly increased β-ODAP titers in all tested pathway combinations.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. All 28 isoleucine codons in codon-optimized LsBAHD were replaced by rare AUA codons. 2. This translation-rate engineering improved soluble protein accumulation about seven-fold. 3. Replacement of LsBAHD with LsBAHD* substantially enhanced β-ODAP production, demonstrating that co-translational folding was a major bottleneck.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Synonymous codon engineering; SDS-PAGE solubility quantification; pathway comparison in feeding experiments
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: engineered solution to rate-limiting terminal step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hemostatic plant metabolite; platelet aggregation promoter; reported therapeutic relevance to diabetic nephropathy
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates a general translation-tuning strategy for improving heterologous plant enzyme function in microbes.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding and de novo shake-flask cultures
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a native gene but an engineered synonymous-codon variant treated here as a core pathway construct because it is central to experimental results.
